items.fact: The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 November 1949 and came into effect on 26 January 1950.
Fundamental Rights are guaranteed in Part III of the Constitution of India.
Directive Principles of State Policy are listed in Part IV of the Constitution.
The Parliament of India consists of the President, Lok Sabha, and Rajya Sabha.
India has 28 states and 8 union territories.
Rajasthan is the largest state in India by area.
Uttar Pradesh is the most populous state in India.
The Ganga is the national river of India.
The Bengal tiger is the national animal of India.
The Indian peacock is the national bird of India.
The lotus is the national flower of India.
Jana Gana Mana is the national anthem of India.
Vande Mataram is the national song of India.
India gained independence on 15 August 1947.
Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister of independent India.
Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India.
Mahatma Gandhi is known for leading India's freedom struggle through non-violence and satyagraha.
The Dandi March (Salt March) was led by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930.
The Quit India Movement was launched in 1942.
The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place in Amritsar in 1919.
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.
The Supreme Court of India is the highest judicial authority in the country.
The Election Commission of India conducts free and fair elections.
Panchayati Raj provides local self-governance in rural India through a three-tier system.
The Green Revolution increased food grain production in India using high-yield seeds and irrigation.
The White Revolution transformed India into one of the largest milk producers.
ISRO is India's national space agency and is known for missions like Chandrayaan and Mangalyaan.
Chandrayaan-3 achieved a successful soft landing near the Moon's south polar region in 2023.
The southwest monsoon is critical for agriculture and water resources in India.
The Himalayas influence India's climate and act as a natural barrier in the north.
India is bounded by the Indian Ocean in the south, Arabian Sea in the west, and Bay of Bengal in the east.
India has alluvial, black, red, laterite, mountain, and desert soil types.
Rice and wheat are major food crops in India; cotton and jute are important cash crops.
The Indus Valley Civilization is one of the earliest urban civilizations in the Indian subcontinent.
The Mauryan Empire reached great heights under Emperor Ashoka.
The Gupta period is often called the Golden Age of ancient India.
The Delhi Sultanate ruled large parts of India before the Mughal period.
The Mughal Empire significantly influenced architecture, administration, and culture in India.
Bhakti and Sufi movements emphasized devotion, equality, and spiritual reform.
The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 and played a major role in freedom movement.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is India's central bank.
Goods and Services Tax (GST) is a unified indirect tax system in India.
Digital India is a flagship program to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
